claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:

**How old is your child?** 

The eligibility rules differ depending on whether your child is:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old
- 20 years or older

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, please let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for your help. I'm inquiring about my two children actually. Alex is 18 and Blake is 13.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children:
- **Alex** (18 years old)
- **Blake** (13 years old)

I'll help you check eligibility for both of them. Let me start with Blake since they're under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (13 years old):**

Is Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

Since Blake is only 13, they're likely not working full-time, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't work at all - they're only 13!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That makes sense. Let me continue with Blake's assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (13 years old):**

Is Blake receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, this is unlikely for a 13-year-old, but I need to check.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (13 years old):**

Is Blake in any type of apprenticeship program? (This is unlikely at age 13, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't doing an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your eligibility as the claimant. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, jobseeking, sufficient resources, studying, or family member of EEA/Swiss national), OR
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? The most common is simply that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I live abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you live abroad, let me check if you qualify under one of the special circumstances.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant** (for example, working for the UK government, armed forces, or diplomatic service)? 

Or **do you live in a qualifying country** where UK Child Benefit can still be claimed? These are typically:
- Gibraltar
- Certain European countries under specific bilateral agreements

Could you tell me:
1. What is your employment situation?
2. Which country do you currently live in?

This will help me determine if you meet the residency requirements despite living abroad.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just live abroad with my family - I'm not working for the UK government.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on what you've told me, let me assess the situation for both of your children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

### **Summary for Your Children:**

**Alex (18 years old): Not Eligible**
- While we didn't complete the full assessment for Alex's individual circumstances, the claim cannot proceed because you don't meet the residency requirements.

**Blake (13 years old): Not Eligible**
- Blake meets the child-specific criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), but the claim cannot proceed because you don't meet the residency requirements.

### **Why You're Not Eligible:**

The main issue is that **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**. To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying conditions), OR
- Be a Crown servant living abroad, OR
- Live in certain qualifying countries

Since you live abroad and don't fall into any of the special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

### **What You Could Do:**

- If you move back to the UK and normally live there, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time
- If your circumstances change (for example, if you become a Crown servant), you may then qualify
- If someone else who meets the residency requirements has responsibility for your children in the UK, they may be able to claim instead

I understand this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, please don't hesitate to reach out.

reasonings: Alex (18 years old) cannot qualify for Child Benefit because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, being a Crown servant abroad, or living in certain qualifying countries. Since the claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant or in a qualifying situation, they do not meet the residency requirements, making them ineligible to claim for Alex.

Blake (13 years old) cannot qualify for Child Benefit because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. Although Blake meets the child eligibility criteria (under 16, not working full-time, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship), the claimant must satisfy the residency requirements to make a claim. Since the claimant lives abroad and does not meet any of the special circumstances (Crown servant, settled status, etc.), they are ineligible to claim for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children (Alex, 18, and Blake, 13) but is living abroad and does not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. While both children may meet the child-related eligibility criteria, the claimant themselves must normally live in the UK or meet specific exceptional circumstances (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). Since the claimant does not meet any of these residency requirements, they are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either child.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
